subject: UX & Research
title: The Therac-25 Radiation Overdose
description: One-minute skill: The Therac-25 Radiation Overdose.

hook.text: Between 1985 and 1987, a radiation machine overdosed six patients. Three died. The interface was the cause.

buildup.text: The Therac-25 let operators type commands so fast that the machine couldn't process corrections in time.

discovery.text: Error messages were cryptic single words: 'MALFUNCTION.' Operators didn't understand and pressed on.

twist.text: Software interlocks were removed because earlier models had hardware safety. The new model relied only on code.

climax.text: Clear error messages, input rate limiting, and hardware interlocks were all missing. The UI failed lethally.

punchline.text: Cryptic errors kill trust — and sometimes people. Be clear.

quiz.question: What interface failures contributed to the Therac-25 disasters?
options: Cryptic error messages that operators couldn't understand and missing safety interlocks
The screen was too small to read
The machine was operated by untrained staff
